content.full_text:
City of Alameda California

[illegible]
FEB 11 8 [illegible]32

February 6, 1992

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel
Legal Division
California Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Re: Request for Formal Written Advice for City Officials and
Staff Regarding Actions Related to Bay Farm Island
Reclamation District No. 2105

Dear Mr. Hallabrin:

On behalf of various City officials and staff, I am writing to
request formal written advice from the FPPC pursuant to Government
Code Section 83114(b) on the official's and staffs' ability to
participate in decisions of the Alameda City Council related to Bay
Farm Island Reclamation District No. 2105. John Wallace of your
office and I discussed the basic issues two days ago. The
conclusion was that a conflict probably existed regarding the
maintenance assessment hearing matters.

The City of Alameda is approximately 9.5 square miles in area.
The population is 76,459 according to the 1990 census data. Bay
Farm Island Reclamation District No. 2105 ("District") includes
less than one seventh of the City's population and is located on a
portion of Bay Farm Island which is separate from the main island.
I have attached a map of the area for your convenience.

The District was established to construct reclamation works
including a perimeter dike and interior drainage system. Funding
for the work was financed by bonds which were paid off by assessing
property within the District's boundaries. Construction of the
work has been completed. Pursuant to its contracts with the City
and permits from Bay Conservation and Development Commission, the
District is required to maintain its reclamation works. In order
to accomplish the maintenance, the District is proposing to
establish a maintenance assessment on property within the District.

Before the property can be assessed for the maintenance work,
the California Water Code requires the City to take several

Office of the City Attorney, Room 314

City Hall
Santa Clara Avenue at Oak Street · 94501
415.748.4544

Scott Hallabrin
February 6, 1992
Page 2


actions. First, after receiving a petition from the District, the
City Council is required to appoint three valuation commissioners.
Water Code Section 51322. (This step has already been done. The
potentially impacted official and staff did not participate in the
decision or its process.) Second, after the valuation
commissioners have determined the proposed assessments and the
assessment roll is forwarded to the City Council, the Council sets
a time for hearing objections to the assessments. Water Code
Section 51329. Third, at the hearing the City Council considers
any written objections and hears evidence supporting the
objections. The City Council may modify the assessments. Water
Code Section 51332. Fourth, the City Council orders approval of
the assessments or modified assessments. Water Code Section
51332.1.

The Mayor, City Manager, City Attorney and Finance Director
all own homes in the District and are subject to any potential
assessments. They have abstained to date from any action regarding
the maintenance assessments including the appointment of the
valuation commissioners. I am asking on their behalf to what
extent these city officials may participate in future actions
related to the hearing on the maintenance assessments, i.e. the
second, third and fourth steps outlined above.

I would appreciate your response as soon as possible. In
order to meet the County's deadline for items to be included on the
tax rolls, the City Council must take action on these reclamation
district issues no later than May 19, 1992. If you need any
additional information, please feel free to contact me at (510)
748-4544. Thank you for your assistance.

Very Truly Yours,

Heather-C. McLaughlin
Assistant City Attorney

enclosure

cc: Mayor and City Council
    City Attorney
    City Manager
    Assistant City Manager
    Finance Director
